element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Bird's-eye view allows player to plan before committing to swing
Player learns view shift between planning and execution phases
Bird's-eye view phase allows club cycling before commitment
Environmental variable adds complexity after basic swing mastery
Advanced shot shaping added after wind effects understood
Distinct mechanical phase isolates putting skill from full swing
Princess Toadstool/Daisy provides contextual information at each hole
Player must master initial course before accessing new difficulty/terrain
International release features simplified hole layouts; Japan release maintains original difficulty

typical_relief_triggers: Bird's-eye planning phase before each shot
typical_tension_triggers: Power meter timing window requiring precision
Late holes in course with increased difficulty
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based pacing with decision-heavy planning phases followed by real-time execution phases; tension builds through hole progression within each course; relief cycles with successful hole completion

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Multiplayer second player (Luigi) available alongside primary player (Mario) in compatible modes

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introductory holes establish swing mechanics and bird's-eye planning phase; basic club selection without complex environmental variables
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Wind and terrain effects introduced; hole layouts become less forgiving; ball spin mechanics required for optimal play
difficulty_curve.late_game: Full mastery of all mechanics required; Japan release maintains significantly higher difficulty with more holes (90 vs 54) and original unmodified layouts
notable_spikes: Course progression unlock requirement (Japan release) creates artificial difficulty gate
International release simplifies hole layouts to reduce late-game difficulty compared to Japan version

notable_level_patterns.description: Every full shot follows consistent rhythm: bird's-eye planning view → third-person swing execution view → result evaluation. Putting uses separate top-down perspective entirely, creating distinct mechanical phase.

Japan release requires sequential course unlocking, forcing mastery before advancement. International release reduces this by simplifying hole difficulty and cutting courses from 5 to 3, lowering barrier to completion.
Wind effects present from early holes but become more punishing as course progression advances; ball spin options expand tactical depth mid-progression
